description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a gable roof and a single chimney on one side. The front facade features four tall, narrow windows with white trim and a single entry door accessed by a small set of steps. The brickwork is uniform and reddish-brown, with subtle decorative elements above the windows. The rear side of the house has a similar window arrangement but with one window bricked up and the door positioned on the opposite side. Both lateral sides are plain, with no windows or doors, emphasizing the house's rectangular footprint. The roof is covered in dark gray shingles, and the overall design is simple and functional, typical of late 19th to early 20th-century urban residential architecture.
description: A two-story brick house with a gable roof, chimney, and symmetrical window arrangement, featuring a simple entryway and classic urban design.
description_view_0: Front view showing four tall windows, a single entry door with steps, and decorative brickwork above the windows.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain brick wall with no windows or doors and the gable roof's slope.
description_view_2: Rear view with three windows (one bricked up), a door on the opposite side from the front, and a lighter lower facade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to the other side, with a plain brick wall and visible roof slope.
attribute_entryway_design: Single door with small steps, no porch, simple white trim, and no overhang
